title: GR/IR quantity mismatch for a PO
desc: Your most-used query. Detects when goods received and invoice received quantities don't match on a PO item.
code: SELECT EBELN, EBELP, SUM(CASE WHEN BEWTP='E' THEN MENGE ELSE 0 END) AS gr_qty, SUM(CASE WHEN BEWTP='Q' THEN MENGE ELSE 0 END) AS ir_qty FROM EKBE WHERE MANDT='100' AND EBELN='4500012345' GROUP BY EBELN, EBELP HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN BEWTP='E' THEN MENGE ELSE 0 END) <> SUM(CASE WHEN BEWTP='Q' THEN MENGE ELSE 0 END)
raw:
SELECT EBELN, EBELP,
  SUM(CASE WHEN BEWTP='E' THEN MENGE ELSE 0 END) AS gr_qty,
  SUM(CASE WHEN BEWTP='Q' THEN MENGE ELSE 0 END) AS ir_qty
FROM EKBE
WHERE MANDT='100'
  AND EBELN='4500012345'
GROUP BY EBELN, EBELP
HAVING
  SUM(CASE WHEN BEWTP='E' THEN MENGE ELSE 0 END) <>
  SUM(CASE WHEN BEWTP='Q' THEN MENGE ELSE 0 END)
why: BEWTP='E' = GR entry, 'Q' = IR entry. SUM handles partial GRs and reversals automatically. HAVING filters to only mismatched lines. Remove the HAVING clause to see all PO items.

title: All stock movements for a material
desc: Trace every goods movement for a material in a plant — essential for stock reconciliation and finding unexplained stock differences.
code: SELECT H.MBLNR, H.BUDAT, H.USNAM, I.BWART, I.MENGE, I.MEINS, I.LGORT, I.EBELN, I.EBELP FROM MKPF AS H INNER JOIN MSEG AS I ON H.MBLNR = I.MBLNR AND H.MJAHR = I.MJAHR WHERE I.MATNR = '000000000010001234' AND I.WERKS = '1000' AND H.BUDAT BETWEEN '20240101' AND '20241231' ORDER BY H.BUDAT DESC
raw:
SELECT
  H.MBLNR, H.BUDAT, H.USNAM,
  I.BWART, I.MENGE, I.MEINS,
  I.LGORT, I.EBELN, I.EBELP
FROM MKPF AS H
INNER JOIN MSEG AS I
  ON  H.MBLNR = I.MBLNR
  AND H.MJAHR = I.MJAHR
WHERE I.MATNR = '000000000010001234'
  AND I.WERKS = '1000'
  AND H.BUDAT BETWEEN '20240101' AND '20241231'
ORDER BY H.BUDAT DESC
why: Always join MKPF+MSEG on BOTH MBLNR and MJAHR — MBLNR alone is not unique across fiscal years. H.USNAM gives you the user who posted the movement.

title: All open PO items for a vendor
desc: Find all open purchase order line items for a specific vendor — useful before vendor meetings or for reconciliation.
code: SELECT H.EBELN, H.LIFNR, H.BEDAT, I.EBELP, I.MATNR, I.TXZ01, I.MENGE, I.NETPR, I.WERKS FROM EKKO AS H INNER JOIN EKPO AS I ON H.EBELN = I.EBELN WHERE H.MANDT = '100' AND H.LIFNR = '0000100234' AND I.ELIKZ = ' ' -- delivery not complete AND I.LOEKZ = ' ' -- item not deleted AND H.BSTYP = 'F' -- only POs, not contracts ORDER BY H.BEDAT DESC
raw:
SELECT
  H.EBELN, H.LIFNR, H.BEDAT,
  I.EBELP, I.MATNR, I.TXZ01,
  I.MENGE, I.NETPR, I.WERKS
FROM EKKO AS H
INNER JOIN EKPO AS I ON H.EBELN = I.EBELN
WHERE H.MANDT = '100'
  AND H.LIFNR = '0000100234'
  AND I.ELIKZ = ' '
  AND I.LOEKZ = ' '
  AND H.BSTYP = 'F'
ORDER BY H.BEDAT DESC
why: ELIKZ=' ' = delivery not marked complete. LOEKZ=' ' = item not deleted. BSTYP='F' filters to Purchase Orders only (excludes contracts, RFQs). TXZ01 is the short item text.

title: Invoice vs PO price variance
desc: Find invoices where the unit price differs from the PO price — the most common cause of blocked invoices.
code: SELECT R.BELNR, R.EBELN, R.EBELP, P.NETPR AS po_price, I.DMBTR/I.MENGE AS inv_price, (I.DMBTR/I.MENGE) - P.NETPR AS variance FROM RSEG AS R INNER JOIN RBKP AS I ON R.BELNR=I.BELNR AND R.GJAHR=I.GJAHR INNER JOIN EKPO AS P ON R.EBELN=P.EBELN AND R.EBELP=P.EBELP WHERE R.MANDT = '100' AND ABS((I.DMBTR/I.MENGE) - P.NETPR) > 0.01 ORDER BY ABS(variance) DESC
raw:
SELECT
  R.BELNR, R.EBELN, R.EBELP,
  P.NETPR             AS po_price,
  I.DMBTR/I.MENGE  AS inv_price,
  (I.DMBTR/I.MENGE) - P.NETPR AS variance
FROM RSEG AS R
INNER JOIN RBKP AS I ON R.BELNR=I.BELNR AND R.GJAHR=I.GJAHR
INNER JOIN EKPO AS P ON R.EBELN=P.EBELN AND R.EBELP=P.EBELP
WHERE R.MANDT = '100'
  AND ABS((I.DMBTR/I.MENGE) - P.NETPR) > 0.01
ORDER BY ABS(variance) DESC
why: DMBTR is invoice total in local currency. Dividing by MENGE gives unit price. ABS filter removes floating-point noise near zero. ORDER BY ABS(variance) shows biggest discrepancies first.

title: Current stock by material and plant
desc: Snapshot of unrestricted, quality, and in-transit stock per material and storage location.
code: SELECT MATNR, WERKS, LGORT, LABST AS unrestricted, INSME AS quality_insp, EINME AS in_transit, SPEME AS blocked_stock FROM MARD WHERE MANDT = '100' AND WERKS = '1000' AND MATNR = '000000000010001234' AND (LABST > 0 OR INSME > 0 OR EINME > 0)
raw:
SELECT
  MATNR, WERKS, LGORT,
  LABST  AS unrestricted,
  INSME  AS quality_insp,
  EINME  AS in_transit,
  SPEME  AS blocked_stock
FROM MARD
WHERE MANDT = '100'
  AND WERKS = '1000'
  AND MATNR = '000000000010001234'
  AND (LABST > 0 OR INSME > 0 OR EINME > 0)
why: LABST=unrestricted, INSME=quality inspection, EINME=in-transit (STO), SPEME=blocked. The OR filter shows storage locations with any stock type. Remove MATNR filter to see all materials.

title: Find who changed a PO and what changed
desc: Audit trail for PO changes — price, quantity, vendor — using SAP change document tables.
code: SELECT H.UDATE, H.UTIME, H.USERNAME, P.FNAME AS field_changed, P.VALUE_OLD, P.VALUE_NEW FROM CDHDR AS H INNER JOIN CDPOS AS P ON H.CHANGENR = P.CHANGENR WHERE H.MANDT = '100' AND H.OBJECTCLAS = 'EINKBELEG' AND H.OBJECTID = '4500012345' ORDER BY H.UDATE DESC, H.UTIME DESC
raw:
SELECT
  H.UDATE, H.UTIME, H.USERNAME,
  P.FNAME  AS field_changed,
  P.VALUE_OLD, P.VALUE_NEW
FROM CDHDR AS H
INNER JOIN CDPOS AS P ON H.CHANGENR = P.CHANGENR
WHERE H.MANDT      = '100'
  AND H.OBJECTCLAS = 'EINKBELEG'
  AND H.OBJECTID   = '4500012345'
ORDER BY H.UDATE DESC, H.UTIME DESC
why: OBJECTCLAS='EINKBELEG' = purchasing document change log. OBJECTID = PO number (10-digit, zero-padded). Shows every field change with old/new values — the forensic tool for procurement disputes.

title: Parked invoices not yet posted
desc: Find all parked (held) invoices — a common source of period-end accruals and payment delays.
code: SELECT BELNR, GJAHR, LIFNR, BLDAT AS invoice_date, BUDAT AS posting_date, XBLNR AS vendor_invoice_no, RMWWR AS gross_amount, BSTAT AS status FROM RBKP WHERE MANDT = '100' AND BSTAT IN ('A', 'V') -- A=parked, V=parked complete AND BUDAT >= '20240101' ORDER BY RMWWR DESC
raw:
SELECT
  BELNR, GJAHR, LIFNR,
  BLDAT  AS invoice_date,
  BUDAT  AS posting_date,
  XBLNR  AS vendor_invoice_no,
  RMWWR  AS gross_amount,
  BSTAT  AS status
FROM RBKP
WHERE MANDT = '100'
  AND BSTAT IN ('A', 'V')
  AND BUDAT >= '20240101'
ORDER BY RMWWR DESC
why: BSTAT='A' is parked (incomplete), 'V' is parked complete (held). BSTAT='B' is posted. RMWWR is gross invoice amount. ORDER BY amount DESC shows highest-value blocked invoices first.

title: Materials below reorder point
desc: Find materials where current unrestricted stock has fallen below the reorder point — useful for procurement planning.
code: SELECT D.MATNR, D.WERKS, D.MINBE AS reorder_point, S.LABST AS current_stock, D.MINBE - S.LABST AS shortage FROM MARC AS D INNER JOIN MARD AS S ON D.MATNR=S.MATNR AND D.WERKS=S.WERKS WHERE D.MANDT = '100' AND D.WERKS = '1000' AND D.MINBE > 0 AND S.LABST < D.MINBE ORDER BY shortage DESC
raw:
SELECT
  D.MATNR, D.WERKS,
  D.MINBE  AS reorder_point,
  S.LABST  AS current_stock,
  D.MINBE - S.LABST AS shortage
FROM MARC AS D
INNER JOIN MARD AS S
  ON D.MATNR=S.MATNR AND D.WERKS=S.WERKS
WHERE D.MANDT = '100'
  AND D.WERKS = '1000'
  AND D.MINBE > 0
  AND S.LABST < D.MINBE
ORDER BY shortage DESC
why: MARC.MINBE is the reorder point. MARD.LABST is unrestricted stock. This join gives you a shortage report — largest gaps first. Only materials with a reorder point set are included (MINBE > 0).

title: GR/IR clearing account open items
desc: Find GR/IR clearing account entries that haven't been matched — these drive your period-end accruals.
code: SELECT E.EBELN, E.EBELP, SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='E' THEN E.MENGE ELSE 0 END) AS gr_qty, SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='Q' THEN E.MENGE ELSE 0 END) AS ir_qty, SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='E' THEN E.WRBTR ELSE 0 END) AS gr_value, SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='Q' THEN E.WRBTR ELSE 0 END) AS ir_value, P.NETPR, P.WERKS FROM EKBE AS E INNER JOIN EKPO AS P ON E.EBELN=P.EBELN AND E.EBELP=P.EBELP WHERE E.MANDT='100' AND P.WERKS='1000' GROUP BY E.EBELN, E.EBELP, P.NETPR, P.WERKS HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='E' THEN E.WRBTR ELSE 0 END) <> SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='Q' THEN E.WRBTR ELSE 0 END)
raw:
SELECT
  E.EBELN, E.EBELP,
  SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='E' THEN E.MENGE ELSE 0 END) AS gr_qty,
  SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='Q' THEN E.MENGE ELSE 0 END) AS ir_qty,
  SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='E' THEN E.WRBTR ELSE 0 END) AS gr_value,
  SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='Q' THEN E.WRBTR ELSE 0 END) AS ir_value,
  P.NETPR, P.WERKS
FROM EKBE AS E
INNER JOIN EKPO AS P ON E.EBELN=P.EBELN AND E.EBELP=P.EBELP
WHERE E.MANDT='100' AND P.WERKS='1000'
GROUP BY E.EBELN, E.EBELP, P.NETPR, P.WERKS
HAVING
  SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='E' THEN E.WRBTR ELSE 0 END) <>
  SUM(CASE WHEN E.BEWTP='Q' THEN E.WRBTR ELSE 0 END)
why: WRBTR is the value in document currency. This query shows both quantity AND value mismatches — critical for finance because even if qty matches, price variance can leave the GR/IR account open.

title: Duplicate invoice detection
desc: Find cases where the same vendor invoice number was entered more than once — prevents double payment.
code: SELECT LIFNR, XBLNR AS vendor_invoice_no, COUNT(*) AS count, SUM(RMWWR) AS total_amount FROM RBKP WHERE MANDT = '100' AND BSTAT = 'B' -- only posted invoices AND XBLNR != '' -- must have vendor ref AND BUDAT >= '20240101' GROUP BY LIFNR, XBLNR HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY total_amount DESC
raw:
SELECT
  LIFNR, XBLNR AS vendor_invoice_no,
  COUNT(*) AS count,
  SUM(RMWWR) AS total_amount
FROM RBKP
WHERE MANDT  = '100'
  AND BSTAT  = 'B'
  AND XBLNR  != ''
  AND BUDAT  >= '20240101'
GROUP BY LIFNR, XBLNR
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY total_amount DESC
why: XBLNR is the vendor's reference number (their invoice number). Same vendor + same invoice number appearing twice = likely duplicate. SAP has a duplicate check but it can be overridden — this SQL catches what slipped through.

title: POs created by a specific user
desc: Find all purchase orders created by a specific user — useful for audits, access reviews, or when a buyer leaves.
code: SELECT H.EBELN, H.LIFNR, H.BEDAT, H.ERNAM AS created_by, SUM(I.MENGE * I.NETPR) AS po_value FROM EKKO AS H INNER JOIN EKPO AS I ON H.EBELN = I.EBELN WHERE H.MANDT = '100' AND H.ERNAM = 'RAJAT.K' AND H.BSTYP = 'F' AND H.BEDAT >= '20240101' GROUP BY H.EBELN, H.LIFNR, H.BEDAT, H.ERNAM ORDER BY H.BEDAT DESC
raw:
SELECT
  H.EBELN, H.LIFNR, H.BEDAT,
  H.ERNAM AS created_by,
  SUM(I.MENGE * I.NETPR) AS po_value
FROM EKKO AS H
INNER JOIN EKPO AS I ON H.EBELN = I.EBELN
WHERE H.MANDT = '100'
  AND H.ERNAM = 'RAJAT.K'
  AND H.BSTYP = 'F'
  AND H.BEDAT >= '20240101'
GROUP BY H.EBELN, H.LIFNR, H.BEDAT, H.ERNAM
ORDER BY H.BEDAT DESC
why: EKKO.ERNAM is the user who created the PO. MENGE*NETPR gives item value — SUM gives total PO value. Change ERNAM to your username to audit your own POs.

title: Slow-moving stock (no movement in 90 days)
desc: Identify materials with stock on hand but no goods movement in the last 90 days — a key inventory management KPI.
code: SELECT D.MATNR, D.WERKS, D.LGORT, D.LABST AS stock_qty, MAX(H.BUDAT) AS last_movement FROM MARD AS D LEFT JOIN MSEG AS I ON D.MATNR=I.MATNR AND D.WERKS=I.WERKS AND D.LGORT=I.LGORT LEFT JOIN MKPF AS H ON I.MBLNR=H.MBLNR AND I.MJAHR=H.MJAHR WHERE D.MANDT='100' AND D.WERKS='1000' AND D.LABST > 0 GROUP BY D.MATNR, D.WERKS, D.LGORT, D.LABST HAVING MAX(H.BUDAT) < ADD_DAYS(CURRENT_DATE, -90) OR MAX(H.BUDAT) IS NULL
raw:
SELECT
  D.MATNR, D.WERKS, D.LGORT,
  D.LABST AS stock_qty,
  MAX(H.BUDAT) AS last_movement
FROM MARD AS D
LEFT JOIN MSEG AS I
  ON D.MATNR=I.MATNR AND D.WERKS=I.WERKS AND D.LGORT=I.LGORT
LEFT JOIN MKPF AS H
  ON I.MBLNR=H.MBLNR AND I.MJAHR=H.MJAHR
WHERE D.MANDT='100'
  AND D.WERKS='1000'
  AND D.LABST > 0
GROUP BY D.MATNR, D.WERKS, D.LGORT, D.LABST
HAVING MAX(H.BUDAT) < ADD_DAYS(CURRENT_DATE, -90)
   OR MAX(H.BUDAT) IS NULL
why: LEFT JOIN keeps materials with no movements at all (NULL last_movement). ADD_DAYS(CURRENT_DATE,-90) is HANA syntax for 90 days ago. This query is the foundation of dead stock reporting.

title: Top 10 vendors by PO value this year
desc: Quick spend analysis by vendor — useful for management reporting and vendor consolidation discussions.
code: SELECT TOP 10 H.LIFNR, MAX(V.NAME1) AS vendor_name, SUM(I.MENGE * I.NETPR) AS total_po_value, COUNT(DISTINCT H.EBELN) AS po_count FROM EKKO AS H INNER JOIN EKPO AS I ON H.EBELN = I.EBELN INNER JOIN LFA1 AS V ON H.LIFNR = V.LIFNR WHERE H.MANDT = '100' AND H.BSTYP = 'F' AND YEAR(TO_DATE(H.BEDAT)) = YEAR(CURRENT_DATE) AND I.LOEKZ = ' ' GROUP BY H.LIFNR ORDER BY total_po_value DESC
raw:
SELECT TOP 10
  H.LIFNR,
  MAX(V.NAME1)             AS vendor_name,
  SUM(I.MENGE * I.NETPR) AS total_po_value,
  COUNT(DISTINCT H.EBELN)  AS po_count
FROM EKKO AS H
INNER JOIN EKPO AS I ON H.EBELN = I.EBELN
INNER JOIN LFA1 AS V ON H.LIFNR = V.LIFNR
WHERE H.MANDT = '100'
  AND H.BSTYP = 'F'
  AND YEAR(TO_DATE(H.BEDAT)) = YEAR(CURRENT_DATE)
  AND I.LOEKZ = ' '
GROUP BY H.LIFNR
ORDER BY total_po_value DESC
why: YEAR(TO_DATE(BEDAT)) is HANA syntax to extract year from date string. TOP 10 is HANA-specific (use LIMIT 10 in standard SQL). Joining LFA1 adds vendor name to make the report readable.

title: Subquery: POs with GR but no IR
desc: Find PO items that have been goods-received but no invoice has been posted yet — your accrual list.
code: SELECT EBELN, EBELP, SUM(MENGE) AS gr_qty, SUM(WRBTR) AS gr_value FROM EKBE WHERE MANDT = '100' AND BEWTP = 'E' -- GR entries only AND EBELN NOT IN ( SELECT DISTINCT EBELN FROM EKBE WHERE MANDT = '100' AND BEWTP = 'Q' -- IR entries ) GROUP BY EBELN, EBELP HAVING SUM(MENGE) > 0 ORDER BY gr_value DESC
raw:
SELECT EBELN, EBELP,
  SUM(MENGE) AS gr_qty,
  SUM(WRBTR) AS gr_value
FROM EKBE
WHERE MANDT = '100'
  AND BEWTP = 'E'
  AND EBELN NOT IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT EBELN
    FROM EKBE
    WHERE MANDT = '100'
      AND BEWTP = 'Q'
  )
GROUP BY EBELN, EBELP
HAVING SUM(MENGE) > 0
ORDER BY gr_value DESC
why: Subquery finds all POs that have ANY IR entry. NOT IN excludes those — leaving POs with GR but zero IR. HAVING SUM(MENGE)>0 excludes fully reversed GRs. This list drives your month-end accrual journal.
